2604.08294.title: Can Vision Language Models Judge Action Quality? An Empirical Evaluation
2604.08294.paper_summary: Action Quality Assessment (AQA) has broad applications in physical therapy, sports coaching, and competitive judging. Although Vision Language Models (VLMs) hold considerable promise for AQA, their actual performance in this domain remains largely uncharacterised. We present a comprehensive evaluation of state-of-the-art VLMs across activity domains (e.g. fitness, figure skating, diving), tasks, representations, and prompting strategies. Baseline results reveal that Gemini 3.1 Pro, Qwen3-VL and InternVL3.5 models perform only marginally above random chance, and although strategies such as incorporation of skeleton information, grounding instructions, reasoning structures and in-context learning lead to isolated gains, none is consistently effective. Analysis of prediction distributions uncovers two systematic biases: a tendency to predict correct execution regardless of visual evidence, and a sensitivity to superficial linguistic framing. Reformulating tasks contrastively to mitigate these biases yields minimal improvement, suggesting that the models' limitations go beyond these biases, pointing to a fundamental difficulty with fine-grained movement quality assessment. Our findings establish a rigorous baseline for future VLM-based AQA research and provide an actionable outline for failure modes requiring mitigation prior to reliable real-world deployment.
2604.08294.paper_summary_zh: 行動品質評估（AQA）在物理治療、運動教練和競賽評判中有廣泛的應用。儘管視覺語言模型（VLMs）對於 AQA 擁有相當大的潛力，但它們在這一領域的實際表現仍然大部分未被特徵化。我們對當前最先進的 VLMs 在活動領域（例如健身、花式滑冰、跳水）、任務、表現和提示策略方面進行了全面評估。基準結果顯示，Gemini 3.1 Pro、Qwen3-VL 和 InternVL3.5 模型的表現僅略高於隨機機會，儘管如納入骨架信息、基於指令、推理結構和上下文學習等策略能帶來孤立的增益，但沒有一種策略是一致有效的。對預測分佈的分析揭示了兩種系統性偏見：一是無論視覺證據如何，傾向於預測正確執行；二是對表面語言框架的敏感性。對任務進行對比性重新表述以減輕這些偏見僅帶來微小的改善，這表明模型的限制超出了這些偏見，指向了對於細粒度運動品質評估的根本困難。我們的發現為未來基於 VLM 的 AQA 研究建立了一個嚴謹的基準，並提供了一個可行的輪廓，以應對在可靠的實際部署之前需要減輕的失效模式。
2604.08294.author: Miguel Monte e Freitas et.al.
2604.08294.authors: Miguel Monte e Freitas, Rui Henriques, Ricardo Rei, Pedro Henrique Martins
